name: Chateau de Mauvezin
summary: A restored medieval castle and Gaston Febus museum that can break up Pyrenees driving without becoming a full-day commitment.
whyItMatters: It is a practical cultural detour in the Hautes-Pyrenees rather than another major-city museum, and it fits the Arreau/Tourmalet geography better than Bordeaux or Barcelona-only ideas.
ticketOrReservationNeed: Ticket likely required; guided visits may vary by season.
pros: Manageable stop near Pyrenees transfer routes
Medieval architecture and local Bigorre/Beam museum context
More weather-resilient than a purely scenic mountain stop
cons: Requires a car detour and exact route planning
Hours may be seasonal or affected by private events

logistics.opening_hours: Official castle site should be checked for July 2026 hours.
logistics.reservation_needed: Probably not for standard self-guided visit, but verify.
logistics.distance_from_base: About 40-60 minutes from Arreau in normal driving conditions, route dependent.
logistics.distance_from_viewing_point: Not near the Tourmalet summit itself; better as a transfer-day stop.
logistics.parking: Likely on-site/nearby village parking; verify with official site.
logistics.road_closure_notes: Avoid assuming Stage 6 race-day access until closures are known.

timing.best_visit_window: Pyrenees arrival/departure day, not the Tourmalet viewing day.
timing.race_day_risk: Medium if attempted on Stage 6 day; low on a separate transfer day.

nearbyFoodDrinkPairing: Pair with lunch in Mauvezin/Capvern or continue toward Bagneres-de-Bigorre depending on route.

sources.title: Chateau de Mauvezin official site
sources.note: Official visitor source for opening, tickets, and programs.
